codexObjective: Implement the CPU-friendly autonomous research loop plan in .omx/plans/ralplan-cpu-friendly-autonomous-research-loop.md using .omx/ultragoal/goals.json as the durable story ledger; commit each story and preserve public-only/no-trade safety.

goals.title: CPU-friendly model registry contract
goals.objective: Add config schema and model registry contract for CPU-friendly model families. Include unknown/GPU/deep-learning model rejection tests.
goals.commitIntent: Constrain autonomous models to CPU-friendly registry
acceptanceCriteria: Config accepts only approved CPU-friendly model families plus legacy baseline where needed.
Config rejects LSTM/Transformer/GPU/unknown model names.
Model params remain public/config-only and forbidden private keys are rejected.

goals.title: CPU walk-forward score model families
goals.objective: Implement equal-weight and sign-weight walk-forward score models under the existing artifact contract. Tests prove out-of-sample scoring and artifact metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Weighted/equal/sign model families share the walk-forward no-leakage contract.
Artifacts expose the selected model_type and fold metadata.
Tests cover model family behavior and pipeline propagation.

goals.title: Config-only candidate generator
goals.objective: Add a deterministic candidate generator for factor/model/window/hyperparameter templates with budget and config-only mutation guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate generation is deterministic for a fixed base config.
Every candidate validates through ExperimentConfig and mutates only config fields.
Candidate budget limits the returned list and includes model/factor/window variation.

goals.title: Real walk-forward candidate evaluator
goals.objective: Wire candidate configs into the reusable public Binance/fake-adapter walk-forward evaluator and isolate artifacts by candidate run_id.
goals.commitIntent: Evaluate autonomous candidates with walk-forward backtests
acceptanceCriteria: Each candidate runs through the public-data walk-forward evaluator with isolated output directories.
Fake adapter tests prove deterministic end-to-end evaluation without API keys.
No candidate evaluation uses in-sample-only shortcuts.

goals.title: Autonomous ledger and feedback summary
goals.objective: Extend ledger/summary output to record candidate artifact lineage, metrics, decision, rationale, and Pareto ranking.
acceptanceCriteria: Ledger records hypothesis, config diff, artifact lineage, metrics, Pareto rank, decision, and rationale per candidate.
Summary identifies the best/deferred candidates without investment-advice claims.
Artifact lineage is reproducible from manifest/report/dashboard paths.

goals.title: Agent loop CLI and docs
goals.objective: Add a run-agent-loop CLI and README usage for the CPU-friendly autonomous loop.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI can run the autonomous loop with public Binance data and no credentials.
README documents CPU-friendly autonomy, safety limits, and example commands.
CLI help/tests cover the new command surface.

goals.title: Final safety and reproducibility gate
goals.objective: Run deterministic tests, a small real public Binance smoke where possible, no-trade safety scan, cleanup/slop scan, code review, and final push.
goals.commitIntent: Verify autonomous research loop safety and reproducibility
acceptanceCriteria: Full deterministic pytest suite passes.
A small public Binance smoke run is attempted and evidence is recorded.
Safety scan finds no private/signed/order routes or trading code.
Final quality gate evidence is written to .omx/ultragoal/final-quality-gate.json.
